commit.message:
fix(devcontainer): generate project-specific machine-id

Generate /etc/machine-id from project folder name hash to maintain
consistent container identity across rebuilds while ensuring different
projects have different IDs.

Format: md5sum of "devcontainer-${localWorkspaceFolderBasename}"

commit.message:
fix(devcontainer): create .claude.json to prevent auth re-confirmation

Add creation of ~/.claude/.claude.json in postCreateCommand.
Without this file, Claude Code treats the environment as a new
installation and requests re-authentication on every container rebuild.

Refs #8

refactor(images): migrate artwork images to Supabase Storage and remove local copies

Remove 280 local artwork images (~414MB) from public/images/artworks/ since all
artwork images are now served from Supabase Storage. Update fallback content files
(artworks-batches) to reference Supabase URLs instead of local filenames.

- Backfill all 345 artworks with WEBP variants in Supabase (thumb/card/detail/hero/original)
- Update 262 artwork image paths in content batch files to Supabase URLs
- Add verify-artwork-images.js script for image accessibility verification
- Add update-content-image-urls.js script for batch URL migration
- Build output reduced from ~700MB to ~118MB (next-image-export-optimizer)

commit.message:
audit: harden backend + improve frontend accessibility and maintainability

Infrastructure:
- Move StartLimitIntervalSec/Burst to [Unit] section (systemd v255+ on Ubuntu 24.04)

Backend:
- Add try/except around json.loads() for prev_ports/prev_tech in scheduler.py
- Add migration 016: index on login_attempts.failed_at for query performance
- Add daily cleanup job (03:30 UTC) purging login_attempts > 24h
- Harden get_checks_config() with try/except and key validation (models.py)
- Add webhook retry (1 retry after 2s on 5xx/timeout) in _deliver()
- Add /auth/refresh endpoint for silent JWT renewal

Frontend:
- Extract shared SkuIcon component (was duplicated in 6 files)
- Add ErrorBoundary wrapping the entire app (App.tsx)
- Add role="dialog", aria-modal, Escape key handler to PricingModal, ProfileModal, OnboardingWizard
- Remove console.error leak in NewsletterWidget.tsx
- Auto-refresh JWT every 20 min in AuthContext

Tests:
- Update test_database.py for migration 016 + legacy table schema

fix: 카카오톡 리스트형 → 텍스트형으로 변경 (3개 짤림 해결)

카카오톡 list 템플릿은 채팅창에서 3개만 미리보기로 노출되는
API 제한이 있음. send_text로 전환하여 전체 종목이 보이도록 수정.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add query result cache for repeated lookups (#20)

Cache lookup() results keyed on all search parameters. LRU eviction at
32 entries. Eliminates redundant BM25/embedding/RRF computation when the
same query is re-issued within the same index lifetime (common in
elaboration workflows and MCP tool retries).

fix: update to March 2026 data, Middle East map center, flight-first card layout

Worker.js — KNOWN_ONGOING updated for March 2026 reality:
- TLV: CRITICAL→HIGH — many airlines resumed post-Jan 2025 Gaza ceasefire
- BEY: CRITICAL→HIGH — airlines returning after Nov 2024 Lebanon ceasefire
- SAA: CRITICAL (unchanged) — still closed, zero commercial flights
- Gaza (GZA/null): NEW entry — airspace = combat zone, airport destroyed
- BGW: HIGH (updated language — describes what IS currently operating)
- DAM: CRITICAL→HIGH — post-Assad transition, recovery phase
- Russia/UUWW: NEW entry — airspace closed to 150+ Western carriers since 2022
- IKA: HIGH (updated — current sanctions + closure risk)
- ADE: HIGH (updated — Houthi campaign current status)
- New field: current_flights_impacted — names specific airlines/routes NOW
- AI prompt: asks for CURRENT disruptions, flight-route level, specific airlines

index.html:
- Map center: [20,0] (Atlantic) → [28,42] (Middle East), zoom 2→3
- Light mode adaptive tiles: CartoDB light_all when light-mode active
- Card redesign: flight-first — shows current_flights_impacted prominently
  above the AI summary with blue accent border (was buried in small meta row)
- airport-header-right: IATA + name + tags in column layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[DOCS] Update DATA_DICTIONARY with Sessions 108-111 column specs (#828)

* [DOCS] Update DATA_DICTIONARY with Sessions 108-111 column specs

- dim_league: 2 → 22 cols (multi-level, branding, location, commissioner)
- dim_season: 9 → 25 cols (rule_set FK, status machine, playoffs, NORAD cols deprecated)
- dim_schedule: 42 → ~31 cols (period scores extracted, standings removed, status/type FKs)
- fact_gameroster: 25 → 18 cols (pure bridge, stats removed, new leadership/line cols)
- fact_registration: 19 → 25 cols (composite PK, NORAD → custom_fields, skill rating generalized)
- fact_draft: 15 → 12 cols (composite PK, NORAD cols → custom_fields, draft_config via rule_set)
- fact_game_status: marked SPLIT → fact_game_pipeline_status + qa_game_audit
- fact_playergames: marked DEPRECATED (redundant with fact_gameroster)

New tables (13):
- dim_division (13 cols) — self-referencing org hierarchy
- dim_game_status (8 cols) — game lifecycle lookup
- dim_game_type (8 cols) — game classification lookup
- dim_league_rule_set (72 cols) — master game rules, replaces ETL hardcodes
- dim_league_rule_set_periods (10 cols) — per-period rule overrides
- dim_team_history (8 cols) — SCD2 team name history
- fact_cv_game_session (12 cols) — CV processing metadata
- fact_game_context (158 cols, 3 phases) — pre-game context
- fact_game_period_scores (8 cols) — per-period scoring
- fact_game_pipeline_status (14 cols) — ETL/CV tracking
- fact_standings_snapshot (12 cols) — point-in-time standings
- fact_team_division (8 cols) — team-division bridge
- qa_game_audit (12 cols) — automated game quality audit

commit.message:
refactor(shell): rename cv-builder → resume-builder across all packages

Eliminates the cv-builder namespace to prevent routing confusion.
All AppType literals, MF remote keys, env vars, agent classes, Redux
actions, and file name updated to resume-builder / ResumeBuilder.

- AppType: 'cv-builder' → 'resume-builder' (appRegistrySlice, settingsSlice, ui/types)
- MF remote key: cv_builder → resume_builder (vite.config, AppFrame, settings-loaders)
- Env var: VITE_REMOTE_CV_BUILDER / CV_BUILDER_API_URL → RESUME_BUILDER_* equivalents
- Agent: CvBuilderDomainAgent → ResumeBuilderDomainAgent; file renamed to resume-builder-agent.ts
- DomainType: 'cv-builder' → 'resume-builder' (meta-orchestrator, chat route, domain-registry)
- Redux: CvBuilderSettings → ResumeBuilderSettings; updateCvBuilderSettings → updateResumeBuilderSettings
- SubAppUrls.cvBuilderApi → resumeBuilderApi (meta-orchestrator, frame-agent-manager)

commit.message:
feat: add LoRA for parameter-efficient fine-tuning (#16)

* feat: add LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) for efficient fine-tuning

Implement LoRALinear module wrapping nn.Linear with low-rank A/B
matrices (Hu et al. 2021). B is zero-initialized so initial output
matches the base model. Includes apply_lora() for targeting attention
and/or FFN layers, merge_lora() for inference, and lora_parameters()
for saving adapter weights. 15 tests covering shapes, initialization,
targeting, forward passes, trainable count, and merge correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: freeze all non-LoRA params after apply_lora

Freeze entire model then selectively unfreeze lora_A/lora_B keys
so only LoRA matrices are trainable. Previously embedding and
other non-targeted layers remained trainable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

user should not need to fill in Entry, Stop, Target, Thesis, or request

since the Mar 9 retrain.
